title: Amend Clone Flow with an Editable Local Demo Sandbox and Feature Introduction Replay
topic: How should the approved Clone Flow be amended so users can edit a persistent local Demo sandbox and reopen the high-level feature introduction without weakening Firebase, myTrips, Offline Cache, or production safety boundaries?
objective: Create a human-approved Decision Amendment and execution-disabled revised Assignment plan. This phase performs no product implementation.

knownFacts: Original Gate 1 approved an owner-only Clone MVP.
Original six-Assignment Gate 2 plan was never approved for execution.
Human requested an editable local Demo sandbox and a feature-introduction replay entry.
Human confirmed the proposed amended behavior.
Existing AppSettingsMenu already exposes a trip-contextual 功能導覽 action.
Existing FeatureTour targets TripDetail controls and is not the same as the high-level feature introduction.
No product implementation, production Firebase access, Merge, or Deploy is authorized.
This Amendment Session supersedes the original clone-demo-architecture-pilot Gate 2 Assignment Plan without changing the original Session.
This Amendment does not revoke the original Clone Flow owner-only, allowlist, feature-flag, Emulator-only, no-Rules, no-production, no-dependency, no-migration, or no-deploy safety requirements.
The original six Assignments remain immutable audit history and must not execute.
Only the nine revised execution-disabled Assignments in this Session are eligible for future Revised Gate 2 review.
decisions: ADR-001: Built-in Demo does not write Firebase.
ADR-002: Built-in Demo is excluded from myTrips.
ADR-006: Original owner-only Clone architecture is approved; its first six-Assignment Gate 2 plan was never approved for execution.
Human amendment confirmation: use an editable persistent local Demo Sandbox and add replayable high-level Feature Introduction entries.
The editable-demo-sandbox-amendment Decision was approved for revised execution-disabled Assignment planning only.

constraints: The original clone-demo-architecture-pilot Session and its six Assignments are immutable audit history and must not execute.
The source Demo template remains immutable; editing applies only to a defensive local Sandbox copy.
Demo open, edit, persistence, and reset must not write Firebase, myTrips, Offline Trip Cache, or Storage.
Clone retains owner-only, allowlist, disabled-by-default feature flag, Emulator-only technical development, and separate Production Gate requirements.
Feature Introduction replay must remain distinct from the existing trip-contextual Feature Tour and must not change onboarding eligibility.
All revised Assignments remain execution-disabled until a separate Revised Gate 2 approval.
Do not modify product code, E2E, Firebase Rules, packages, dependencies, migrations, production Firebase, or deployment state in this phase.
questions: How should the immutable Demo template produce and reset a persistent editable Sandbox without mutation leakage?
Which itinerary and Checklist edits are supported while expenses, settlements, tickets, attachments, and Storage stay preview-only?
How should versioned localStorage validation, corruption recovery, memory fallback, and reset confirmation behave?
How must Clone validate and transform the current Sandbox snapshot while preserving every approved Clone safety boundary?
How should Lobby and Settings replay the high-level Feature Introduction without changing first-run state or merging it with the trip-contextual Feature Tour?
Which revised non-overlapping Assignments and regression evidence are required before conditional Merge can be considered?
